figures.caption: Figure 1: Diff-prior calibrates NRI graph inference. (a) A factorized prior can yield diffuse edge posteriors and an ambiguous graph. (b) Diff-prior denoises and calibrates encoder logits before discretization, producing sharper edge posteriors and a more decisive graph.

Figure 2: Diff-prior overview and training objective. We learn a diffusion-parameterized, non-factorized prior over encoder logits 𝑍0 and inject it before discretization to calibrate NRI edge posteriors, then decode dynamics from the sampled graph. Training minimizes a reconstruction loss together with a diffusion denoising loss using a noise-estimation network.
Table 1: Main results (AUROC, %). Diff-prior is plugged into different backbones across network families and dynamics. Bold indicates the best performance.
Table 3: Posterior diagnostics and calibration under NRI: average entropy and ECE (10 bins) of edge posteriors. Bold indicates the best (lowest) value.

Figure 3: Confidence distributions of edge posteriors under the NRI backbone across five network families and two dynamics. Each subplot shows the complementary CDF (1−CDF) of confidence max(𝑝, 1−𝑝); higher curves near 1 indicate a larger fraction of high-confidence predictions.
Table 4: Calibration on the 10% lowest-entropy test samples under the NRI backbone. Lower ECE and NLL indicate better calibrated and more reliable confident predictions.
Table 9: Training time per epoch (in seconds) under different graph priors (Uniform, Fixed, and Diff-prior) across five datasets.
